concepts.title: Sum of two squares
concepts.blurb: Prime splitting in Gaussian integers characterizes n=x^2+y^2.

concepts.title: Gaussian integers and prime splitting
concepts.blurb: Primes p≡1 mod 4 split in Z[i]; p≡3 mod 4 stay inert; p=2 ramifies — the structural reason behind Fermat's theorem.

concepts.title: Legendre three-square theorem
concepts.blurb: Integers not of form 4^a(8b+7) are sums of three squares.

concepts.title: Four squares and Waring perspective
concepts.blurb: Lagrange and Waring-type refinements show universal additive quadratic behavior.

concepts.title: Waring's problem: g(k) and G(k)
concepts.blurb: Hilbert's theorem guarantees finitely many k-th powers suffice; g(k) and G(k) sharpen the count asymptotically.

concepts.title: Waring's problem: g(k)
concepts.blurb: For each k, g(k) is the least s such that every n is a sum of s k-th powers: g(2)=4 (Lagrange), g(3)=9 (Wieferich), g(4)=19.

concepts.title: g(k) versus G(k)
concepts.blurb: G(k) is the asymptotic Waring constant (sufficiently large n); G(k) ≤ g(k), often strictly, and G(k) = O(k log k).

concepts.title: Hilbert–Waring theorem
concepts.blurb: Hilbert (1909) proved g(k) is finite for every k — the first abstract proof, by a combinatorial identity, later superseded by the circle method.

concepts.title: Circle method setup
concepts.blurb: Count representations as a Fourier integral: r_{s,k}(N) = ∫₀¹ f(α)^s e(−αN) dα with f(α)=Σ e(αn^k), then split the unit interval into major and minor arcs.

concepts.title: Major arcs vs minor arcs
concepts.blurb: Near rationals a/q with small q, f(α) is a Gauss-sum-weighted main term (major arcs); elsewhere Weyl bounds force cancellation (minor arcs) — the sum yields an asymptotic with singular series × singular integral.

concepts.title: Faulhaber's formula for power sums
concepts.blurb: The sum $\sum_{k=1}^{n} k^m$ is a polynomial in $n$ of degree $m{+}1$, with coefficients assembled from binomial numbers and Bernoulli numbers.

concepts.title: Bernoulli numbers and their generating function
concepts.blurb: The Bernoulli numbers $B_n$ arise from $t/(e^t-1)=\sum B_n t^n/n!$; all odd $B_n$ beyond $B_1$ vanish and the even ones alternate in sign.

concepts.title: Euler's formula for $\zeta(2n)$
concepts.blurb: Euler expressed $\zeta(2n)$ in closed form using Bernoulli numbers; the negative-integer values $\zeta(-n)=-B_{n+1}/(n+1)$ are the shadow seen through the functional equation.

concepts.title: Euler-Maclaurin summation
concepts.blurb: Euler-Maclaurin turns a finite sum into an integral plus endpoint corrections weighted by Bernoulli numbers, making asymptotic expansions of slowly-converging series accessible.

concepts.title: Functional equation preview via Mellin
concepts.blurb: The completed zeta $\xi(s)=\pi^{-s/2}\Gamma(s/2)\zeta(s)$ satisfies $\xi(s)=\xi(1-s)$; the Bernoulli-number values on both sides are the first consistency check of the reflection.
